unit: PR1 — Create plugins/commons/ skeleton + marketplace cascade entries

contract.goal: Establish the commons plugin's substrate identity: a content-empty plugin tree at plugins/commons/ that the marketplace cascade declares as a dependency of every eventual consumer (griot, guild, loom, ev, agent-loop-full). No content moves in this PR; PR2 extends the sync script to learn the commons→consumer direction; PR3 starts moving canonical content from root into commons. The 'foundation' is laid before any structural change.
acceptance_criteria: plugins/commons/.claude-plugin/plugin.json exists with {name: 'commons', description: <role-naming copy>}. No 'version' field (every-commit-auto-version posture per RESEARCH § 11). Description names the role (foundation substrate; cross-cutting helpers) rather than enumerating inventory — survives day-1 emptiness honestly.

.claude-plugin/marketplace.json registers commons as the 7th plugin. griot/guild/loom/ev each gain `commons` as their FIRST dep (substrate-first ordering convention per design-systems whiteboard finding). agent-loop-full's cascade list adds commons. agent-loop-full's description updates to include commons in the family enumeration.

cli/marketplace-manifest.test.ts: (a) EXPECTED_PLUGIN_NAMES list grows to 7 closed-set entries (NOT loosened to 'any name' — skeptic finding); (b) commons-as-first-dep assertion added for griot/guild/loom/ev; (c) substrate-dep assertions split from peer-dep assertions (visible-in-CI-output semantic distinction per design-systems finding); (d) lines 53-60 + 201-209 mechanically updated for the new 7-plugin shape.

scripts/sync-shared.ts PLUGINS list adds 'commons'. PLUGIN_CONTENT_RULES adds a 'commons' entry as content-empty placeholder. No sync-direction extension in this PR (that's PR2). No PLUGINS_WITH_CLI change (commons ships no CLI of its own).
README.md 'What's inside' table line 13-22 reflects 7 plugins. agent-loop-full's plugin description in the README also updated.
`npm test` passes. `node scripts/sync-shared.ts --check` passes (no new drift; commons content-empty means no new sync activity).

Manual smoke note in notes_for_pr: ran `claude plugin install agent-loop-full@krambuhl --scope user` on a fresh dir OR documented why this couldn't be done in this session (e.g., 'V4-style smoke deferred to pre-merge; cascade test invariant covers static manifest correctness'). The smoke gap is acknowledged per testing-strategy whiteboard finding.
rules_applied: Closed-set 'exactly 7 plugins' manifest tripwire stays closed (skeptic finding).
Substrate-first dependency ordering convention (design-systems finding): commons listed FIRST in each consumer's deps array.
Role-naming description, not inventory (design-systems finding): plugin description survives content-emptiness.
No content into plugins/commons/ at PR1 (PR3-PR5+ own content moves). The plugin tree contains plugin.json only — no bin/, no skills/, no cli/, no docs/.
No sync logic extension in PR1 (PR2 owns that). Only the PLUGINS list and PLUGIN_CONTENT_RULES entry land here.

Per the cascade-wiring interview decision (option a, confirmed post-whiteboard): wire all 5 eventual consumers at PR1 even though commons is content-empty. review-skill is excluded (its content folds INTO commons at PR8).
Family-shape consistency (substrate-engineer finding): commons plugin.json matches the other 5 — {name, description} only. No version field. No version-special-case carve-out.
disqualifiers: Any content (skills, agents, lib, docs, bin) inside plugins/commons/ beyond plugin.json itself. PR1 is skeleton-only.
Any modification to scripts/sync-shared.ts beyond extending PLUGINS and PLUGIN_CONTENT_RULES (sync-direction extension belongs to PR2; planner refactor belongs to PR2).
Loosening the closed-set 'exactly N plugins' manifest invariant in marketplace-manifest.test.ts.
Any plugin-name reference still saying 'shared' in marketplace.json or plugin.json. The rename must be consistent across all wired files this PR touches.
Cascade dependency added to review-skill (it's already going away at PR8) or commons listed in some-other-than-first position in any consumer's deps array.
Skipping the V4-style smoke check WITHOUT acknowledging the gap in notes_for_pr.
inputs: projects/2026-05-21-repo-compartmentalize/PLAN.md (§ Phase 1 — Setup, PR1 description, post-whiteboard revision)
projects/2026-05-21-repo-compartmentalize/RESEARCH.md (§ 7 grill-me + shared plugin, § 8 review-skill over-compartmentalization, § 9 agent-loop-full justified, § 11 plugin.json shape consistency)
projects/2026-05-21-repo-compartmentalize/whiteboards/1-setup.md (round 1 — substrate-engineer, skeptic, design-systems, testing-strategy, performance findings)
cli/marketplace-manifest.test.ts (lines 38-51 plugin.json shape; 53-60 EXPECTED_PLUGIN_NAMES; 181-209 declared dependency edges — all targets of extension)
scripts/sync-shared.ts (lines 39-52 PLUGINS list; 69-102 PLUGIN_CONTENT_RULES — targets of extension)
README.md (lines 13-22 plugin table; lines 97-126 What's inside — targets of edit)

actions: Created plugins/commons/.claude-plugin/plugin.json (3 lines). Description names the role ('Foundation substrate: cross-cutting helpers…') rather than inventory, per design-systems whiteboard finding.

Updated .claude-plugin/marketplace.json: added commons entry at index 0; declared cascade deps with commons listed FIRST for griot/guild/loom/ev/agent-loop-full (substrate-first ordering). Updated agent-loop-full description to enumerate commons in the family. Updated top-level marketplace description to mention commons as a family member.
Updated plugins/agent-loop-full/.claude-plugin/plugin.json description to include commons in the cascaded family list.

Updated cli/marketplace-manifest.test.ts: EXPECTED_PLUGIN_NAMES grew from 6 to 7 entries (commons first); 'exactly the six expected plugins' → 'exactly the seven expected plugins'; dependency edge tests rewritten for the new 7-plugin shape (griot/guild now depend on commons only; loom depends on commons+guild+griot; ev depends on commons+loom+guild+griot; agent-loop-full depends on all 6); NEW describe block 'marketplace manifest: substrate-first dependency ordering' fires 5 assertions one per consumer (commons must be deps[0]).

Updated scripts/sync-shared.ts: PLUGINS const grew from 6 to 7 with commons first (substrate-source ordering); added a content-empty PLUGIN_CONTENT_RULES entry for commons. No sync-logic extension in this PR (PR2 owns the planner refactor).
Updated README.md: 'six self-contained Claude Code plugins' → 'seven'; plugin table grew a commons row at top; cascade-install reference now mentions commons + substrate-first ordering convention.
Ran `node scripts/sync-shared.ts --check` → ok (5 plugins synced; same count as pre-PR since commons + agent-loop-full are both zero-content).

Ran /guild-validate with evaluator-contract-fit + evaluator-react-api + evaluator-test-unit + evaluator-naming. All 4 returned VERDICT: approved. evaluator-naming surfaced one advisory: scripts/sync-shared.ts FILENAME retains the working-name 'shared'; if PR2 reshapes the script's job toward sourcing from commons/, the filename will read literally (named after the old working concept) rather than semantically. Future-cleanup awareness — not in PR1 scope.

changes_since_previous: First unit on this branch. Phase-1 branch (repo-compartmentalize.setup) cut from main after the loom plan + research commits + the PLAN.md revision-from-whiteboard commit landed on main (dc12232). The plan/research commits were subsequently moved off local main to a dedicated `plan-repo-compartmentalize` branch and surfaced via PR #17 (planning-artifacts-via-PR gap fix discussed mid-session).
notes_for_pr: V4-style fresh-machine smoke test was NOT run as part of PR1 (testing-strategy whiteboard finding: 'make it manual and gate it with a checklist line, not a CI job'). The cascade-test invariant in cli/marketplace-manifest.test.ts covers static manifest correctness (commons present in cascade, commons listed first in every consumer's deps, closed-set 7-plugin assertion). Empirical smoke is recommended pre-merge but acceptable to defer; the gap is acknowledged.

Advisory from evaluator-naming: scripts/sync-shared.ts FILENAME retains the working-name 'shared' rather than reflecting the chosen plugin name 'commons'. The script itself works correctly post-PR1 (it just adds 'commons' to its PLUGINS list). If PR2 reshapes the script's job toward sourcing from commons/, consider renaming the file (e.g., scripts/sync-commons.ts or sync-plugins.ts). Out of PR1 scope; future-cleanup awareness.

Per the cascade-wiring interview decision (option a, post-whiteboard confirmed): all 5 eventual consumers (griot/guild/loom/ev/agent-loop-full) gain `commons` as a dependency at PR1, even though commons is content-empty. review-skill is excluded — its content folds INTO commons at PR8 when review-skill the plugin disappears.

Substrate-first ordering convention introduced this PR: commons (substrate-kind dep) listed FIRST in each consumer's `dependencies` array, before peer-kind deps (guild, griot, loom). A new describe block in marketplace-manifest.test.ts asserts this for each consumer; the convention is documented in the README's cascade-install paragraph.
